The outcomes of execution for modality gap calculation experiments match the results in Table 1: all model-only settings still exhibit a high severity of modality gap, and adding batch normalisation (+BN, +BN*) results in a moderate level of modality gap, in which +BN* performs the best with the lowest centroid distance and linear separability metrics.

The outcomes of downstream evaluation experiments match the results in Table 1: LCCO generally achieves higher performance across tasks, while LCCOM exhibits a slight performance decrease alongside a reduced modality gap.

The outcomes of execution for modality gap calculation experiments match the results in Table 2: The I0T variants generally reduce the modality gap, and the I0T_{post} framework exhibits the lowest modality gap on both centroid distance and linear sparability, reaching a low severity of modality gap.

The outcomes of downstream evaluation experiments match the results in Table 2: The I0T variants achieve higher performance than CLOOB and Unif-Align while retaining a low modality gap, and I0T_{post} framework even outperforms all other baselines on retrieval tasks (I2T, T2I).
